items.title: GroWell Cap Review: I Have Hair for the First Time in 15 Years
items.summary: I shaved my head 15 years ago and never looked back. This GroWell LED cap changed that.

items.title: The US Has a Plan to Combat Screwworm. It Involves a Lot More Flies
items.summary: Releasing sterilized flies can crash a local population of flesh-eating screwworms. But the US currently has limited capacity to produce them.

items.title: Logitech G512 X 98 Review: A Hybrid Mish-Mash
items.summary: The Logitech G512 X 98 lets you swap between mechanical and analog switches in an attempt to achieve the best of both worlds. Unfortunately, its solution isn't as well thought out as I’d hoped.

items.title: Reid Hoffman is leaving Microsoft’s board to go ‘founder mode’ with  startup Manus
items.summary: After a very profitable decade on Microsoft's board, Reid Hoffman is stepping down to focus on his AI drug discovery startup Manus.

items.title: Founders share VC horror stories, and some are naming names
items.summary: A massive viral conversation sharing VC horror stories has taken place this week on X. Some are weird. Some are infuriating.

items.title: Former cyber executive turned whistleblower accuses IBM of covering up several data breaches
items.summary: IBM and two of its subsidiary companies were allegedly breached during the mid-2010s — a lawsuit filed by a former cybersecurity executive accuses IBM of not disclosing and actively covering it up.

items.title: Putin says there is 'no point' meeting Zelensky over ending Ukraine war
items.summary: The Russian president's refusal comes after his Ukrainian counterpart wrote an open letter calling for face-to-face talks.

items.title: Ukraine strikes cargo ships and admits Romania drone blast
items.summary: Ukraine says it struck five ships carrying illegal cargo in the Sea of Azov and in coastal waters of Russian-occupied territories.

items.title: Astronauts return to ISS after sheltering during air leak repair attempt
items.summary: Russian attempt to repair tunnel area sparks safe-haven procedure for five other astronauts onboard.

items.title: Hot jobs report puts Fed cuts further out of reach as Chair Warsh faces policy tests
items.summary: Another big jobs report in May has swept aside the possibility of interest rate cuts anytime soon.

items.title: Where investors may find the next 'big wave' for AI trade
items.summary: Tim Urbanowicz, chief investment strategist at Innovator from Goldman Sachs Asset Management, tackles the AI boom.

items.title: China poaches more AI talent from the U.S. as it eyes the next 'super-app'
items.summary: Tencent Chief AI Scientist Yao Shunyu, who joined the company from OpenAI, said Friday he aims to pursue artificial general intelligence.

items.title: Stocks Sink in Broad AI Rout Sparked by China's DeepSeek
items.summary: U.S. stocks were mostly lower, with the Nasdaq leading declines as makers of AI infrastructure suffered steep falls, many in the double digits. Nvidia was down 16%.

items.title: Comex Gold, Silver Settle Lower
items.summary: Gold settled 1.4% lower, down two of the past three sessions, and silver fell 2.5%, down three of the past four sessions.

items.title: DeepSeek Won't Sink U.S. AI Titans
items.summary: Panic fueling the selloff of Nvidia, Broadcom and other tech giants is overblown.

items.title: Trump talks fertilizer, fuel to farmers in Wisconsin, but also Reflecting Pool, fountains
items.summary: President Trump sought to appeal to farmers at a roundtable in Wisconsin on Friday amid skyrocketing fertilizer and energy prices as a result of the U.S. war in Iran. “We’re going to come out, and your fertilizer prices are going to go way down, just like they were four months ago,” Trump told farmers in…

items.title: DOJ prosecutor in California opens ‘multiple election fraud’ probes
items.summary: A federal prosecutor in California has opened probes into alleged voter fraud in the state after President Trump accused Democrats of “cheating” in the primary elections this week. Bill Essayli, the first assistant U.S. attorney for the Central District of California, announced Friday that his office had “multiple election fraud investigations underway” but did not…

items.title: ’60 Minutes’ correspondents say they’ll stick with show
items.summary: Three top correspondents at “60 Minutes” say they will stay with the program amid speculation about their futures as CBS News implements sweeping changes across the show and the network more broadly. In a letter to colleagues, which was obtained by The Hill on Friday, Lesley Stahl, Bill Whitaker and Jon Wertheim wrote they are…

items.title: Blue Origin's explosion just made SpaceX even harder to catch
items.summary: Blue Origin's explosion just made SpaceX even harder to catch

items.title: JPMorgan's Jamie Dimon to flex massive branch network in SpaceX IPO pitch
items.summary: JPMorgan's Jamie Dimon to flex massive branch network in SpaceX IPO pitch

items.title: As AI Semiconductor Revenue Swells, AVGO Stock is Destined to Trend Higher
items.summary: As AI Semiconductor Revenue Swells, AVGO Stock is Destined to Trend Higher

items.title: Giant fire tornadoes could clean up oil spills faster with less pollution
items.summary: Researchers have shown that controlled fire whirls can clean up oil spills faster and more cleanly than traditional burning methods. The spinning flames consumed up to 95% of the oil, cut soot emissions by 40%, and could help prevent spills from reaching sensitive marine habitats.

items.title: Hidden supermassive black hole pairs may finally have a visible signal
items.summary: Scientists have proposed a new method for finding tightly bound supermassive black hole pairs by searching for stars that flash repeatedly as their light is magnified by the black holes’ gravity. The timing and brightness of these bursts could provide a unique fingerprint of black holes slowly spiraling toward a future collision.

items.title: Scientists discover why ozempic may not work for some people
items.summary: Scientists have identified genetic variants that may make some people less responsive to GLP-1 drugs used to treat Type 2 diabetes. Roughly 10% of the population carries these variants, which appear to cause a mysterious form of "GLP-1 resistance." In several clinical trials, carriers were significantly less likely to reach healthy blood sugar targets while taking GLP-1 medications.

items.title: NASA declares MAVEN, its Mars atmosphere orbiter, dead
items.summary: Over more than a decade at Mars, the orbiter revealed how the solar wind strips away the planet’s atmosphere — and why the world lost its water.

items.title: Honeybees and shrimp are now getting vaccinated
items.summary: A shrimp vaccine for commercial use could protect the environment and prove vaccines aren’t just for vertebrates.

items.title: This tiny, blue octopus is new to science
items.summary: The deep-sea octopus is fully mature despite fitting in a palm, a trait researchers think may help it reproduce faster than larger relatives.
